description: Evals for the SecOps ADK agent. Each prompt is a realistic user message sent to the avatar agent, which forwards commands to the SecOps A2A server. Expected tool chains follow SKILL.md decision guide.

evals.name: scan-and-report
evals.prompt: Run a security scan on all monitored repos and give me the report.
evals.expected_output: A Markdown security report written to the workspace. Response summarises critical/high alert counts per repo and the report file path.

assertions.name: calls-fetch-before-report
assertions.description: fetch_security_alerts is called before generate_security_report

assertions.name: report-file-path-in-response
assertions.description: Response contains the path to the written .md report

assertions.name: no-re-scan-skipped
assertions.description: Agent does NOT call get_latest_report instead of fetching fresh data

evals.name: remediate-no-prs
evals.prompt: There are some critical Dependabot alerts in our repos. Please remediate them but don't open PRs yet — I want to review the branches first.
evals.expected_output: Remediation runs with create_prs=False. Response lists repos where branches were pushed, status is pending_pr_approval, and mentions secops_pending_prs.json.

assertions.name: remediate-called
assertions.description: remediate_vulnerabilities is called

assertions.name: create-prs-false
assertions.description: create_prs is NOT set to True (user explicitly said no PRs yet)

assertions.name: pending-prs-mentioned
assertions.description: Response mentions pending PR queue or secops_pending_prs.json

evals.name: remediate-and-open-prs
evals.prompt: Go ahead and remediate all critical vulnerabilities and open the pull requests directly — I trust the patches.
evals.expected_output: remediate_vulnerabilities called with create_prs=True. Response lists pr_url for each repo where a PR was created.

assertions.name: create-prs-true
assertions.description: remediate_vulnerabilities is called with create_prs=True

assertions.name: pr-url-in-response
assertions.description: Response mentions at least one PR URL or pr_created status

evals.name: show-latest-report
evals.prompt: Show me the findings from the last security scan — no need to re-run it.
evals.expected_output: Contents of the saved secops_security_report.md returned without calling fetch_security_alerts.

assertions.name: get-latest-report-called
assertions.description: get_latest_report is called

assertions.name: no-fresh-fetch
assertions.description: fetch_security_alerts is NOT called (user said no re-run)

evals.name: approve-queued-prs
evals.prompt: I reviewed the branches — please go ahead and open the pull requests for the remediation branches that are queued.
evals.expected_output: approve_queued_prs called. Response lists repos and their new PR URLs.

assertions.name: approve-prs-called
assertions.description: approve_queued_prs is called

assertions.name: no-re-remediate
assertions.description: remediate_vulnerabilities is NOT called again (PRs already queued)

evals.name: lookup-cve-by-id
evals.prompt: Can you look up CVE-2023-44487 for me? I want to know the CVSS score and what to do about it.
evals.expected_output: lookup_cve called with cve_id='CVE-2023-44487'. Response includes CVSS score, severity (CRITICAL/HIGH), description of HTTP/2 rapid reset attack, and reference URLs.

assertions.name: lookup-cve-called-with-id
assertions.description: lookup_cve is called with cve_id set to CVE-2023-44487

assertions.name: cvss-score-in-response
assertions.description: Response includes a CVSS base score

assertions.name: no-scan-triggered
assertions.description: fetch_security_alerts is NOT called — this is a standalone CVE lookup

evals.name: lookup-cve-by-package
evals.prompt: What CVEs exist for the requests library version 2.28? We might be using an old version.
evals.expected_output: lookup_cve called with package_name containing 'requests'. Response lists matching CVEs with severity and references.

assertions.name: lookup-cve-by-package-name
assertions.description: lookup_cve is called with package_name, not cve_id

assertions.name: cve-results-returned
assertions.description: Response includes at least one CVE identifier

evals.name: get-allowlist
evals.expected_output: get_allowlist called. Response lists the repos in the allowlist from secops.toml.

assertions.name: get-allowlist-called
assertions.description: get_allowlist is called

assertions.name: no-scan-triggered
assertions.description: fetch_security_alerts is NOT called just for listing repos

evals.name: agent-status
evals.prompt: What's your current configuration — workspace path, SLIM endpoint, which agent ID are you running as?
evals.expected_output: get_agent_status called. Response includes workspace path, slim_endpoint, slim_identity, and allowlist.

assertions.name: get-agent-status-called
assertions.description: get_agent_status is called

assertions.name: workspace-or-endpoint-in-response
assertions.description: Response mentions workspace path or SLIM endpoint

evals.name: scan-then-enrich-cves
evals.prompt: Run a full security scan and then look up any CVEs you find in the NVD so I get the full picture.
evals.expected_output: fetch + generate called first, then lookup_cve called once per unique CVE ID found in alerts. Response integrates CVSS data into the summary.

assertions.name: fetch-before-lookup
assertions.description: fetch_security_alerts is called before lookup_cve

assertions.name: lookup-cve-called
assertions.description: lookup_cve is called at least once

assertions.name: report-generated
assertions.description: generate_security_report is called
